title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Gastroenteritis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms of gastroenteritis. This is because many herbs have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive system. For example, Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When ingested, curcumin can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as nausea and abdominal pain.  Another herb that has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of gastroenteritis. In addition, ginger has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to alleviate stomach cramps and diarrhea.  Licorice root, or Glycyrrhiza glabra, is another herb that has been used for centuries to treat digestive issues. It contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the digestive system. Licorice root tea can help to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as heartburn and indigestion, and can also help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can be a natural and effective way to relieve symptoms of gastroenteritis. Drinking a warm cup of tea can help to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms, such as nausea and abdominal pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help to speed up the recovery process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, along with other bioactive constituents like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and damage to the digestive tract caused by gastroenteritis. The antioxidant properties of curcuma longa essential oils also help neutralize free radicals that can harm the digestive system. Curcumin's ability to modulate the gut microbiome and reduce inflammation makes it effective in alleviating symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment, curcuma longa essential oils can help speed up the recovery process from gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa roots, 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil), a glass jar, and a cheesecloth.
Grind the dried Curcuma longa roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground Curcuma longa powder (1 tablespoon) with the carrier oil (2 cups) in the glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Then, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and store the Curcuma longa essential oil in a glass bottle. Use 5-7 drops per serving, mixed with water, to help relieve gastroenteritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains gingerols and shogaols, which are bioactive constituents with anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. The essential oils also contain gingerol, which has antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of gingerols and shogaols also help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help to alleviate the symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale roots and 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a saucepan with water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 3-4 hours, allowing the roots to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the roots.
Use a steam distiller to extract the essential oil from the infused water, following the manufacturer's instructions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains licorice root compounds that help with gastroenteritis. These compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Glycyrrhizin also has a protective effect on the gastrointestinal tract, preventing damage from stomach acid and enzymes. The flavonoids, such as licochalcone A, have antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gut. This helps to calm the stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and promote healing in the digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1/4 cup of the root powder with 2 cups of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm and dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will allow the essential oil to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids and use the infused oil for gastroenteritis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains active constituents like estragole, limonene, and pinene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the digestive tract. Foeniculum vulgare essential oils also contains antioxidants like beta-pinene, which can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. The oil's carminative properties can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, making it easier for the body to recover from gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help the body heal faster and recover from gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds, a glass jar, a coffee filter, a cheese cloth, a clean glass bottle, and a carrier oil like jojoba oil.
Dry the Foeniculum vulgare seeds in a warm place for 2 weeks. Stir them every day.
Place 1 cup of the dried seeds in the glass jar. Add 2 cups of carrier oil. Close the jar and shake it well.
Leave the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking it every day. Then, strain it through the coffee filter into another jar.
Filter the oil mixture through the cheese cloth into the clean glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains limonene, menthone, and menthol as its active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help fight off the bacteria and viruses causing gastroenteritis. Limonene and menthone also have anti-inflammatory effects, which reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms like diarrhea and stomach cramps. Additionally, menthol helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and killing off pathogens, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Let the liquid cool down to room temperature. Then, place it in the fridge to chill.
Use a dropper to extract the oil from the chilled liquid and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosinic acid, camphor, and bornyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the digestive tract, making it an effective treatment for gastroenteritis. The bornyl acetate in Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils also helps to relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that often accompany gastroenteritis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of the essential oil help to fight off infections and promote healing in the digestive tract. The combination of these properties makes Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils a useful treatment for reducing symptoms and promoting recovery from gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add the fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Use a distillation kit to separate the essential oil from the water and collect 1 tablespoon of the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils contains a compound called quinine, which has been shown to help with gastroenteritis. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. The essential oil also contains alkaloids like cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines. These compounds help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea associated with gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestive calmness, Cinchona officinalis essential oils can provide relief from gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark pieces, a glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Combine the 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark pieces and 2 cups of carrier oil in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into another container, squeezing the cheesecloth to get as much oil as possible.
Store the Cinchona officinalis essential oil in a small glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, keeping it away from heat and light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains bioactive constituents like silymarin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and damage caused by gastroenteritis. Silymarin also has a protective effect on the liver, which can become overworked during an episode of gastroenteritis. Additionally, silymarin has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing symptoms like nausea and vomiting. By reducing inflammation and protecting the digestive system, Silybum marianum essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil)
Clean the flowers thoroughly, then put them in a clean glass jar
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the flowers, then seal the jar
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily to help the oil extract from the flowers
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, then discard the solids

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde and linalool, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacteria and viruses causing gastroenteritis. These properties also reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it easier for the body to recover from the infection. The essential oil's antispasmodic properties help relieve cramps and nausea associated with gastroenteritis, providing relief to the patient. The oil's carminative properties also aid in eliminating trapped gas and reducing bloating, which can exacerbate the condition. By targeting the root causes of gastroenteritis, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum bark, 2 cups of water, and a saucepan.
Combine the Cinnamomum verum bark and water in the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour, then let it sit for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discard the solids.
Use a distillation kit to separate the essential oil from the liquid, then collect the oil in a separate container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene and beta-pinene as its major bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off the infection that causes gastroenteritis. The antispasmodic properties of limonene can also help to calm down stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from the discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of beta-pinene help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can exacerbate the condition. By reducing inflammation, fighting off infection, and calming stomach cramps, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water over medium heat and bring it to a boil. Then, reduce the heat to low.
Add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to the pot and let them simmer for 20-25 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 hours or overnight. Then, use a pipette to transfer the essential oil into a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils contains bioactive constituents like limonene, beta-pinene, and piperine. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat gastroenteritis. The antimicrobial properties of limonene and beta-pinene reduce the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, while piperine helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of these constituents also aid in reducing nausea and vomiting associated with gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of pathogens, Piper nigrum essential oils may provide relief from gastroenteritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal essential oils for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Piper nigrum berries and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat and add the Piper nigrum berries.
Let it simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Combine the strained liquid with 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut oil) in a bowl.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it steep for 2 weeks, shaking the bottle daily.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gastroenteritis, but it's not a guarantee. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. They may also help calm the digestive system, which can help prevent the symptoms of gastroenteritis from forming in the first place.
faq_safe: It's not recommended to drink herbal teas daily for gastroenteritis. While they can help soothe symptoms, overconsumption may worsen issues like dehydration. Herbal teas are meant for occasional use. Drinking them daily can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes and fluids, potentially prolonging recovery. Limit herbal teas to when you feel unwell, then drink water and other fluids to stay hydrated.
faq_timeline: For gastroenteritis, herbal teas like peppermint and ginger can help soothe the stomach. Their effects may be felt within 30 minutes to an hour after consumption. Some people may notice relief from nausea and stomach cramps sooner, while others may take a bit longer to feel better.
faq_time: When you have gastroenteritis, it's best to drink herbal tea on an empty stomach, like first thing in the morning or an hour before eating. This helps calm your stomach and settle any nausea. Avoid drinking tea close to mealtime, as it might make you feel bloated or uncomfortable.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, especially if you have gastroenteritis. Some herbs like peppermint and chamomile can slow down the movement of food through your digestive system, making symptoms worse. This could be a problem if you're already taking medications that are meant to help your body recover from gastroenteritis.
faq_pregnant: If you have gastroenteritis while pregnant, it's best to stick to gentle drinks like water and clear broth. Herbal teas can be tempting, but some may not be safe. Peppermint tea, for example, can cause stomach cramps and may not be good during pregnancy. It's always better to be cautious with what you drink when you're pregnant.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be helpful for gastroenteritis symptoms, but it's essential to check their ingredients. Some herbs can be too strong for breastfeeding women. For example, peppermint and ginger are usually safe, but sage and pennyroyal can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Always read the label and choose teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
